req.title: Approve leave request — variant 4
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path approval by the assigned manager, (2) rejection by a non-assignee manager (403), (3) double-action on an already-approved request (409), (4) rate-limit enforcement, and (5) auto-escalation boundary at exactly 5 business days. Tests are mapped to EP, NEG, STC, DT, and BVA heuristics respectively. Each test uses fixtures and helper placeholders to stay self-contained and idempotent.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_request_succeeds
preconditions: Leave request exists with state=pending and assigned to this manager
Employee account exists and can receive notifications
expected: Audit log entry created for the approval action
trace: R-WEB-065: manager shall approve requests assigned to them
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_approves_assigned_request_succeeds(
    api_client,          # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client for manager role
    pending_leave_request,  # <fixture>: creates & yields a pending request assigned to manager
    audit_log_reader,    # <fixture>: helper to query audit entries
    notification_store,  # <fixture>: helper to query notification records
):
    request_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    employee_id = pending_leave_request["employee_id"]

    resp = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "approved"
    assert body["id"] == request_id

    audit_entries = audit_log_reader.fetch(resource_id=request_id)
    assert any(e["action"] == "approve" for e in audit_entries)

    notifications = notification_store.fetch(recipient_id=employee_id)
    assert any(n["event"] == "leave_approved" for n in notifications)

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403
preconditions: Two manager accounts exist: manager_a (assignee) and manager_b (non-assignee)
Leave request exists with state=pending assigned to manager_a
expected: Response body contains error code indicating forbidden/not-assignee
No audit entry created for manager_b
trace: R-WEB-065 error pathway: 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403(
    api_client_factory,   # <fixture>: factory returning authenticated client for given user
    pending_leave_request_for_manager_a,  # <fixture>: request assigned to manager_a
    manager_b_credentials,  # <fixture>: credentials dict for manager_b
    audit_log_reader,
):
    request_id = pending_leave_request_for_manager_a["id"]
    client_b = api_client_factory(manager_b_credentials)

    resp = client_b.post(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve")

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "not_assignee" in body.get("error_code", "").lower() \
        or body.get("status") == "forbidden"

    audit_entries = audit_log_reader.fetch(
        resource_id=request_id,
        actor=manager_b_credentials["user_id"]
    )
    assert audit_entries == []

tests.name: test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409
preconditions: Leave request exists with state=approved (already actioned by this manager)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve a second time with same manager credentials
expected: Response body indicates conflict / already actioned
trace: R-WEB-065 error pathway: 409 if already actioned
R-WEB-065 state transition: approved -> approve again is invalid
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409(
    api_client,
    approved_leave_request,  # <fixture>: yields request already in state=approved
    audit_log_reader,
):
    request_id = approved_leave_request["id"]

    resp = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve")

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "already" in body.get("message", "").lower() \
        or body.get("error_code") == "already_actioned"

    entries_before = audit_log_reader.count(
        resource_id=request_id, action="approve"
    )
    # only the original approval entry should exist
    assert entries_before == 1

tests.name: test_approve_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold N is known from system configuration
N distinct pending leave requests assigned to this manager exist
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve N times in rapid succession
expected: Response headers include Retry-After or X-RateLimit-Reset
State of the N+1-th request remains unchanged
trace: R-WEB-065: operation must be rate-limited per user
BVA at exact rate-limit boundary N vs N+1
tests.executable:
import pytest
from config import RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD  # <helper_fn>: exposes configured threshold N

def test_approve_rate_limit_exceeded_returns_429(
    api_client,
    pending_leave_requests_bulk,  # <fixture>: creates RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD+1 pending requests
):
    requests_list = pending_leave_requests_bulk  # list of dicts with 'id'
    assert len(requests_list) == RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD + 1

    for req in requests_list[:RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD]:
        resp = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{req['id']}/approve")
        assert resp.status_code == 200, \
            f"Expected 200 for request {req['id']}, got {resp.status_code}"

    overflow_id = requests_list[RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD]["id"]
    resp_overflow = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{overflow_id}/approve")

    assert resp_overflow.status_code == 429
    assert (
        "retry-after" in resp_overflow.headers
        or "x-ratelimit-reset" in resp_overflow.headers
    )

tests.name: test_unactioned_request_auto_escalates_at_5_business_days
preconditions: Leave request exists with state=pending, assigned to manager
Request was created exactly 5 business days ago (boundary value)
Auto-escalation job is configured to run on schedule
actions: Trigger or await the auto-escalation scheduled job
GET /leave-requests/{id} to read current state and assignee
expected: Request state changes to escalated or assignee changes to hr-admin role
Original manager is no longer the sole assignee
Audit entry records the escalation event
trace: R-WEB-065: unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin within 5 business days
BVA at exactly 5 business days (not 4, not 6)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unactioned_request_auto_escalates_at_5_business_days(
    api_client_admin,          # <fixture>: admin-level client to read request state
    stale_pending_request_5bd, # <fixture>: pending request aged exactly 5 business days
    escalation_job_runner,     # <fixture>: triggers auto-escalation job synchronously
    audit_log_reader,
):
    request_id = stale_pending_request_5bd["id"]

    escalation_job_runner.run_now()

    resp = api_client_admin.get(f"/leave-requests/{request_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()

    escalated = (
        body.get("state") == "escalated"
        or body.get("assignee_role") == "hr-admin"
    )
    assert escalated, f"Expected escalation but got state={body.get('state')}"

    audit_entries = audit_log_reader.fetch(resource_id=request_id)
    assert any(e["action"] == "auto_escalate" for e in audit_entries)

tests.name: test_reject_vs_approve_decision_table
preconditions: Two pending leave requests exist: one to approve, one to reject
Both requests are assigned to the authenticated manager
expected: Separate audit entries exist for each action
trace: R-WEB-065: manager shall approve OR reject leave requests
DT: covers both action branches and their distinct state outcomes
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reject_vs_approve_decision_table(
    api_client,
    two_pending_requests,   # <fixture>: yields dict {"approve_req": {...}, "reject_req": {...}}
    audit_log_reader,
    notification_store,
):
    approve_id = two_pending_requests["approve_req"]["id"]
    reject_id = two_pending_requests["reject_req"]["id"]
    employee_id = two_pending_requests["approve_req"]["employee_id"]

    resp_a = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{approve_id}/approve")
    assert resp_a.status_code == 200
    assert resp_a.json()["state"] == "approved"

    resp_r = api_client.post(f"/leave-requests/{reject_id}/reject")
    assert resp_r.status_code == 200
    assert resp_r.json()["state"] == "rejected"

    audit = audit_log_reader.fetch_multiple(
        resource_ids=[approve_id, reject_id]
    )
    actions = {e["action"] for e in audit}
    assert "approve" in actions
    assert "reject" in actions

    notifs = notification_store.fetch(recipient_id=employee_id)
    notif_events = {n["event"] for n in notifs}
    assert "leave_approved" in notif_events
    assert "leave_rejected" in notif_events
